evals.prompt: Screen these search results for a scoping review on rotator cuff repair outcomes in adults. Inclusion: adults (≥18), rotator cuff repair (surgical), clinical outcomes (re-tear rates, functional scores, return to activity). Include systematic reviews, meta-analyses, RCTs, and cohort studies. Exclusion: pediatric populations, animal studies, in vitro/cadaveric studies, editorials, letters, and studies focused on non-rotator-cuff shoulder conditions (e.g., adhesive capsulitis).
evals.expected_output: screening_log.md with title and abstract screening completed, included_indices.json with final indices, PRISMA counts printed. Should include indices 0,1,4,6 (Zhang meta-analysis, Chen SR, Lee SR on diabetes, Nakamura cohort). Should exclude 2 (cadaveric), 3 (pediatric), 5 (animal), 7 (editorial), 9 (adhesive capsulitis). Index 8 (Anderson) is borderline — comorbidity prevalence study, not repair outcomes.
expectations: review/screening_log.md exists and contains 'Status: COMPLETE' for title screening
review/screening_log.md contains 'Status: COMPLETE' for abstract screening
review/included_indices.json exists and is valid JSON
Indices 2 (cadaveric), 3 (pediatric), 5 (animal) are excluded at title screening
Index 7 (editorial) is excluded
Index 9 (adhesive capsulitis) is excluded
Indices 0, 1, 4, 6 are retained in the final included set
included_indices.json contains exactly [0, 1, 4, 6] (no extra indices)
Screening summary is printed with counts at each stage (retained + excluded = pool)
Each exclusion has a specific reason (not just 'irrelevant')

evals.prompt: I need to screen these articles for a systematic review on rotator cuff disease and comorbidities (diabetes, smoking, obesity) in adults. Include: studies examining the relationship between comorbidities and rotator cuff disease outcomes in adults. Study types: systematic reviews, cohort studies, cross-sectional studies. Exclude: case reports, animal studies, studies on non-rotator-cuff conditions, articles not about comorbidities. Note: none of these articles have abstracts in the JSON — please fetch them from PubMed.
evals.expected_output: screening_log.md completed, abstracts fetched from PubMed for articles with PMIDs, combined_results.json backfilled with fetched abstracts. Index 2 (limb salvage tumors) should be excluded. Indices related to comorbidities (5-smoking, 6-prevalence/risk factors, 7-genetic risk factors) should be retained.
expectations: MCP tool fetch_abstracts was called with fetch_missing true
review/screening_log.md exists with title screening marked 'Status: COMPLETE'
review/screening_log.md contains abstract screening section
Index 2 (limb salvage tumors) is excluded with a specific reason
review/included_indices.json exists and is valid JSON
Screening summary is printed with counts
Articles without abstracts after fetch are listed in a 'No Abstract' section or handled appropriately

evals.prompt: Rapid review: screen these results on SGLT2 inhibitors in heart failure. Include: RCTs and meta-analyses on SGLT2 inhibitors with heart failure outcomes (mortality, hospitalization), published 2018-2024. Exclude: studies focused only on diabetes without HF outcomes, practice guidelines, studies before 2018. This is a rapid review so combine title and abstract screening.
evals.expected_output: Combined title+abstract screening in a single pass (rapid review mode). Index 4 (DKA management guideline) excluded. Index 5 (EMPA-REG 2015) excluded by date. Indices 0, 1, 3 retained. Index 2 (Zelniker 2019 meta-analysis on T2DM) is borderline — it's about T2DM cardiovascular outcomes, not specifically HF, but does report HF hospitalization.
expectations: Screening log shows combined title+abstract screening (not separate steps), consistent with rapid review
review/screening_log.md exists with screening marked 'Status: COMPLETE'
Index 4 (DKA practice guideline) is excluded
Index 5 (EMPA-REG 2015) is excluded due to date criterion (before 2018)
Indices 0 and 1 are retained
review/included_indices.json exists and is valid JSON
Screening summary is printed with counts (retained + excluded = pool)
